notableCases: Largest MCTS lift: case 27 +3/10 (+30.0pp), covering WebGL texture format/state policy where code-only missed extension-gated validation and intrinsic video dimensions.
Next largest lift: case 33 +2.5/10 (+25.0pp), where MCTS supplied rejected DisplayList/call-site fork history for Clay graphics backend abstraction.
Strong +2/10 or equivalent lifts: cases 11, 12, 17, 29, 31, 35, 38, and 46 each gained +20.0pp from design-tree/history evidence.
Other meaningful MCTS gains: cases 1, 5, 14, and 32 gained +15.0pp to +16.7pp by adding measured rationale, rejected alternatives, threading, or backend-gate context.
Code-only was already complete or tied in cases 2, 4, 7, 8, 9, 18, 22, 24, 47, and 50.
Code-only was very close within 0.5 points in cases 10, 15, 21, 28, 30, 40, 41, and 43.
MCTS underperformed code-only in case 49 (-1/5, -20.0pp), case 39 (-1/10, -10.0pp), and case 21 (-0.5/10, -5.0pp).
Weighted totals: code-only 430.1/491 = 87.60%; MCTS-aware 474.2/491 = 96.58%; net lift +44.1 points = +8.98pp.
Unweighted per-case averages: code-only 87.74%; MCTS-aware 96.39%; average per-case lift +8.64pp.
Case 48 was completed manually after workflow scoring failed; code-only 4/5, MCTS-aware 5/5.

results.title: DevTool DOM compression as CDP contract and scheduling boundary
results.codeOnlyAnswer: Explains DOM.enable compression negotiation, ordinary JSON compatibility, TASM-vs-DevTool thread split, thresholded zlib/base64 payload wrapping, and CDP-wire framing; it lacks concrete measured motivation.
results.mctsAwareAnswer: Adds design-tree and commit evidence for opt-in CDP compression, async response pipeline, 1.4 MB to 64 KB / 72 ms and 73%-78% transfer-time savings, plus wire-contract rationale.
results.notable: MCTS helped by supplying measured historical motivation.

results.title: Fiber Lepus chunks as keyed TemplateBundle sections
results.codeOnlyAnswer: Fully explains bundle-owned keyed LEPUS_CHUNK sections, route-table random access, __LoadLepusChunk path lookup, lazy/async decode, compatibility, debug identity, and rejection of inline raw bytes.
results.mctsAwareAnswer: Also fully covers MCTS rationale: container/integrity boundary, routed sections, path-keyed chunks, lazy/background/full decode modes, and historical alternatives such as raw Relax bytes or flat sections.
results.notable: Tie; code-only already found the essential architecture and evidence.

results.mctsAwareAnswer: Completes coverage by explicitly tying MTSRuntime/InspectorLepusObserver lifecycle to target naming, session connection, debug-info responsibilities, and historical direct-wrapper replacement.
results.notable: MCTS added the missing target-routing/session details.

results.codeOnlyAnswer: Fully explains WhiteBoard as shared storage and delegates as TASM/runtime callback adapters, rejecting TemplateAssembler ownership and TASM-only paths for standalone runtime.
results.mctsAwareAnswer: Fully explains the same split with design-tree alternatives: direct TemplateAssembler coupling, single TASM delegate path, and TASM/Lepus-specific storage replaced by runtime/Relax reuse pressure.
results.notable: Tie; both answers satisfy all rubric points.

results.mctsAwareAnswer: Adds RFC/MCTS three-stage ResourceLoader fetch, JS evaluation to factory, per-view execution, Node-style failed-entry retry, stale-state pitfalls, and rejected extra ResourceLoader cache layer.

results.title: Owned Starlight not Yoga
results.codeOnlyAnswer: Based only on code and git evidence, the answer should push back on reintroducing Yoga/Yoga-compatible nodes/styles. Current code exposes and implements Starlight as Lynx's layout engine: the standalone C API uses SLNodeRef, SLNodeStyleSet*, SLNodeCalculateLayout, and StarlightMeasureDelegate, and its implementation wraps lynx::starlight::LayoutObject plus LayoutComputedStyle, not Yoga. Git history shows commit fc6e4ec1 titled replace yoga, deleting ReactCommon/yoga/* while adding ReactCommon/starlight/*, plus later Yoga cleanup/removal commits. A new layout feature should be added to Starlight's own style/model/API boundary rather than exporting Yoga semantics. Uncertainty: code/git show Yoga was replaced and removed, but do not fully explain the precise design rationale or reopening condition.
results.mctsAwareAnswer: The design-aware answer should say no by default: Lynx's current design is an owned Starlight layout backend, not Yoga or a Yoga-compatibility facade. The MCTS node records owned Starlight layout backend identity and a renderer-independent layout scheduling/API boundary; its Yoga alternative is frozen in .alt and was replaced by layout-starlight in commit fc6e4ec1 with the recorded reason: owning the engine lets Lynx encode CSS-native style and measurement contracts directly and removes Yoga as a public semantic option. The feature should be designed as Starlight-native style/algorithm/API work, preserving the standalone/API boundary, not by exposing Yoga-compatible nodes/styles or making Yoga flex behavior public. Reopen Yoga only if new evidence shows the old rejection reason has lapsed.
results.notable: Manual completion for missing workflow case 48.

results.mctsAwareAnswer: Strongly covers generated property IDs, typed CSSValue/UnitHandler, rejected string/per-use alternatives, and custom-property exceptions, but underexplains ordered declarations/cascade as a first-class requirement.

results.mctsAwareAnswer: Fully adds MCTS grammar-aware parsing rationale, rejected per-property splitting/fixed-position alternatives, Tailwind pitfalls, parser/tokenizer evidence, narrow split exceptions, and target/feature gates.
results.notable: Tie; both complete.

binaryAllOrNothing.definition: A case receives credit only if the answer receives full rubric score; otherwise it receives 0 for that case.

primaryUnweighted.description: SWE-Atlas-style solved-tasks/total-tasks metric. Each case counts once regardless of rubric max score.

secondaryWeighted.description: Rubric-weighted binary metric. 496 is the sum of per-case max scores; solved cases contribute maxScore, unsolved cases contribute 0.
